[Garnett S. Stokes](/wiki/Garnett_S._Stokes "Garnett S. Stokes") (2023)[[1]](#cite_note-governance-1) President, [University of New Mexico](/wiki/University_of_New_Mexico "University of New Mexico")

[newspapers](https://www.google.com/search?&q=%22Universities+Research+Association%22&tbs=bkt:s&tbm=bks) **·** [books](https://www.google.com/search?tbs=bks:1&q=%22Universities+Research+Association%22+-wikipedia) **·** [scholar](https://scholar.google.com/scholar?q=%22Universities+Research+Association%22) **·** [JSTOR](https://www.jstor.org/action/doBasicSearch?Query=%22Universities+Research+Association%22&acc=on&wc=on) *(September 2025)* *([Learn how and when to remove this message](/wiki/Help:Maintenance_template_removal "Help:Maintenance template removal"))* |

(**URA**) is a non-profit association of more than 90 [research universities](/wiki/Research_universities "Research universities"), primarily but not exclusively in the United States. It has members also in Japan, Italy, and the United Kingdom. It was founded in 1965 at the behest of the [President's Science Advisory Committee](/wiki/President%27s_Science_Advisory_Committee "President's Science Advisory Committee") and the [National Academy of Sciences](/wiki/National_Academy_of_Sciences "National Academy of Sciences") to build and operate [Fermilab](/wiki/Fermilab "Fermilab"), a [National Accelerator Laboratory](/wiki/SLAC_National_Accelerator_Laboratory "SLAC National Accelerator Laboratory").

Consortium of research-oriented universities and colleges

Universities Research Association

|  |  |
| --- | --- |
|  | |
| Formation | 1965 |
| Headquarters | [Washington, D.C.](/wiki/Washington,_D.C. "Washington, D.C."), United States |
| Location | * + United States   + [Italy](/wiki/Italy "Italy")   + [Japan](/wiki/Japan "Japan")   + [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom") |
| Membership | 90 |
| Council of Presidents, Chair | [Garnett S. Stokes](/wiki/Garnett_S._Stokes "Garnett S. Stokes") (2023)[[1]](#cite_note-governance-1) President, [University of New Mexico](/wiki/University_of_New_Mexico "University of New Mexico") |

The **Universities Research Association** (**URA**) is a non-profit association of more than 90 [research universities](/wiki/Research_universities "Research universities"), primarily but not exclusively in the United States. It has members also in Japan, Italy, and the United Kingdom. It was founded in 1965 at the behest of the [President's Science Advisory Committee](/wiki/President%27s_Science_Advisory_Committee "President's Science Advisory Committee") and the [National Academy of Sciences](/wiki/National_Academy_of_Sciences "National Academy of Sciences") to build and operate [Fermilab](/wiki/Fermilab "Fermilab"), a [National Accelerator Laboratory](/wiki/SLAC_National_Accelerator_Laboratory "SLAC National Accelerator Laboratory").

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Universities_Research_Association&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The [President's Science Advisory Committee](/wiki/President%27s_Science_Advisory_Committee "President's Science Advisory Committee") and a sister group of the [United States Atomic Energy Commission](/wiki/United_States_Atomic_Energy_Commission "United States Atomic Energy Commission") joined forces in 1962 to "assess the future needs in high-energy accelerator physics."[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2) The panel's recommendations, issued in 1963, included the need to immediately commence design and construction on 200 [GeV](/wiki/Electronvolt "Electronvolt") [proton accelerators](/wiki/Particle_accelerator "Particle accelerator"). An additional recommendation called for a new administrative construct.[[2]](#cite_note-:0-2)

On January 17, 1965, the National Academy of Sciences addressed the last recommendation by sponsoring a meeting of presidents from 25 research universities to discuss the management of the accelerator facility that would later become the [Fermi National Accelerator Laboratory](/wiki/Fermilab "Fermilab") (Fermilab). The meeting eventually resulted in the decision to form the Universities Research Association, with 34 original members, to build and manage the new accelerator facility. URA filed its articles of incorporation on June 21, 1965. J. C. Warner, president of the [Carnegie Institute of Technology](/wiki/Carnegie_Mellon_University "Carnegie Mellon University"), served as URA's first president.

## Projects

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Universities_Research_Association&action=edit&section=2 "Edit section: Projects")]

URA has helped develop the Tevatron at Fermilab. Its early activities are related to the [Superconducting Supercollider](/wiki/Superconducting_Super_Collider "Superconducting Super Collider"), the [Pierre Auger Observatory](/wiki/Pierre_Auger_Observatory "Pierre Auger Observatory"), the Long-Baseline Neutrino Facility (LBNF), and the associated [Deep Underground Neutrino Experiment](/wiki/Deep_Underground_Neutrino_Experiment "Deep Underground Neutrino Experiment") (DUNE), and involvement in the Honeywell International-led National Technology and Engineering Solutions at Sandia (NTESS) that manages and operates [Sandia National Laboratories](/wiki/Sandia_National_Laboratories "Sandia National Laboratories"). Current major projects of the association include supporting Fermilab through a partnership with the [University of Chicago](/wiki/University_of_Chicago "University of Chicago") and coordinating U.S. support of the Pierre Auger Cosmic Observatory.
